title: asperella gracilis
intro: Asperella gracilis, also known as the delicate earthball or poisonous earthball, is a species of fungus in the family Pyronemataceae. Asperella gracilis contains a compound called asperic acid, which has been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Asperella gracilis include its potential to treat various conditions, such as wounds, skin infections, and even certain types of cancer. Asperella gracilis is traditionally prepared as a tincture or infused oil, which can be used topically or internally, although further research is needed to confirm its safety and efficacy.
